content.problemStatement:
Given a bubbleSort function that accepts an array as a parameter, sort the array using the **Bubble sort** algorithm.

**Note:** Do not write code to take input or print output, Only complete the implementation of the bubbleSort function.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the sorted array in ascending order on a single line, with elements separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The array [6,5,3,1,8,7,2,4] is sorted by repeatedly swapping adjacent out‑of‑order elements, resulting in the ascending order [1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8].
The reverse‑sorted array becomes ascending after bubble sort passes.
A single‑element array is already sorted.

solution.approach: Bubble sort repeatedly steps through the array, compares adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order. After each outer pass the largest unsorted element bubbles to its final position, so the inner loop can ignore the last i elements. The algorithm is simple to implement and works in‑place, requiring only O(1) extra memory. For each test case we run the two nested loops described above and then print the resulting array.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) per test case, because there are two nested loops each up to N.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space, sorting is performed in‑place.
